<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='1',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><a name="PartI"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Part
I</font></b></a></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Item1_"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Item 1.</font></b></a><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Business</font></p>

<p style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">TRC Companies,
Inc. (the Company), incorporated in 1971, is a customer-focused company that
creates and implements innovative solutions to the challenges facing America&#146;s
environmental, energy, infrastructure and security markets.&#160; The Company is a leading provider of
technical, financial, risk management and construction services to industry and
government customers across the country.&#160;
Traditionally, the Company&#146;s work was focused primarily on providing
conventional technical services to commercial and government customers.&#160; In early 1998, new Company management
initiated a growth plan focused on two key areas.&#160; First, while maintaining the traditional
business, the Company embarked on a plan to increase growth in economically
driven markets.&#160; Secondly, it would
provide higher-value, &#147;problem-solving&#148; services and elevate purchasing decisions
in customer organizations from middle to senior management.&#160; This approach has resulted in a superior
growth rate and has allowed the Company to continuously transform its services
to suit the evolving needs of its customers.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Requirements
related to infrastructure (e.g., water, transportation), energy, and the
environment are among the primary constraints to the nation's growth today and
will remain so for the foreseeable future.&#160;
While opportunities for the Company exist virtually everywhere in the country,
in 2004 the Company refined its strategic plan to fully lever its strong market
position by devoting the majority of its resources to geographic areas expected
to provide the largest markets for its services.&#160; These include the high growth regions of
California and Texas, and the adjacent states, and the corridor between
Southern Connecticut and Philadelphia where population growth is modest, but
transformation from industrial to commercial and residential uses is greatest.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company
currently provides services in the following areas:</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Environmental
&#150; </font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company
provides engineering, scientific and technical environmental services to
customers through a national network of over 85 offices.&#160; These services have been one of the Company&#146;s
historic core strengths and serve as the foundation for the Company&#146;s Exit
Strategy&#174; program.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Environmental
services provided by the Company include pollution control, waste management,
auditing and assessment, permitting and compliance, design and engineering, and
natural and cultural resource management.&#160;
The Company has particular expertise in air quality, emissions control
and monitoring; in licensing new and expanded facilities; and in investigating
and cleaning up environmentally impaired sites.&#160;
While these services are generally required by the Company&#146;s customers
for compliance with federal, state and local environmental laws, the Company is
experiencing an increase in its business due to: (1) the rehabilitation of
older business processes to increase efficiency and productivity; (2) mergers,
acquisitions and divestitures, and the identification, quantification and
resolution of environmental liabilities associated with past practices; and (3)
the redevelopment of former industrial properties to meet changing demographic
patterns.&#160; The Company believes these
economically motivated projects, as well as enforcement of environmental laws
and regulations, will continue to provide opportunities to expand and grow its
traditional environmental business.&#160; In
addition, the Company has an information management group that assists its
customers by analyzing their data management requirements and creating software
and hardware solutions for cost-effective information management systems.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has
developed a unique, innovative method for its customers to outsource their
environmental remediation activities to the Company.&#160; The Company&#146;s Exit Strategy program provides
its customers with a cost-effective alternative to managing their non-core
environmental remediation activities.&#160;
This is especially attractive to customers in the following situations:</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Mergers, Acquisitions and
Divestitures</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> - In
typical transactions, neither buyer nor seller wishes to be responsible for
existing environmental conditions of transferred assets.&#160; The Company solves this dilemma by
identifying and quantifying the risk and then entering into a contract with
both buyer and seller to complete the remediation.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Discontinued Operations</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> - When the Company&#146;s customers close
facilities or purchase redundant facilities as part of a business transaction,
they are faced with the environmental legacy of the property.&#160; By outsourcing the management of these
responsibilities to the Company, our customers can re-allocate resources to
ongoing business operations.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Multi-Party Superfund Sites</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> - By law, the cleanup of abandoned,
environmentally impaired sites is the responsibility of those businesses that
sent waste materials to the site during its period of operation.&#160; The typically inefficient and lengthy process
of negotiating and managing these cleanups drives up the legal and
administrative cost burden for the responsible parties.&#160; By taking sole responsibility for the site,
the Company eliminates the additional cost and expedites the schedule&nbsp;for
cleanup.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Brownfields Real Estate Development </font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">- The redevelopment of commercially
viable but environmentally contaminated property is commonly referred to as
&#147;brownfields&#148; redevelopment.&#160; The Company
has, on occasion, purchased contaminated property from its customers and is in
the process</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.5pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='2',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">of cleaning up these properties to increase their market value for
eventual sale or redevelopment.&#160; The
Company does not actively pursue these sites on its own but generally seeks a
real estate development partner with experience in the local or regional real
estate market.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Regardless of the
type of Exit Strategy project undertaken, the Company completes a thorough due
diligence process to understand and quantify the environmental condition of the
property.&#160; The Company then designs a
specific risk management plan to address the known and unknown risks.&#160; This plan includes insurance to adequately
protect the Company and its customers from future risks.&#160; The Company&#146;s insurer on these projects is
the American International Group (AIG).</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Through its Exit
Strategy program, the Company is recognized as a leader in the environmental
remediation outsourcing market, and expects the market for these projects will
continue to grow.&#160; The Company has an
experienced team of technical and management personnel to continue to develop
these opportunities.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Energy /
Power </font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#150; The
Company has traditionally provided a variety of technical services to energy
and electrical power customers from offices across the country.&#160; In 1998, the Company determined that changes
in the energy industry would create substantial new demand for high quality,
responsive services.&#160; Since that time the
Company has established a leadership position in supporting the licensing of
large electric generating facilities and re-powering older plants in areas of
the country with the highest demand for additional power.&#160; In addition, the Company is providing a
variety of technical services in support of large scale wind-powered electric
generating projects and hydroelectric facilities.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has
substantially expanded its energy and power services to meet the growing demand
and need for reliable energy. As discussed below, the Company has established a
national capability for permitting, engineering and construction management
services for the natural gas and electric transmission and distribution
markets.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The proven
long-term supply of natural gas is enabling industry to expand its use of this
cleaner fuel source.&#160; This increased
demand is in turn fostering expansion of the country&#146;s gas pipeline
transmission system.&#160; The Company has one
of the leading practices supporting this infrastructure expansion.&#160; The Company is also assisting customers who
are developing a new generation of liquid natural gas (LNG) facilities that
will provide needed capacity to meet peak demand cycles.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As the 2003
blackouts in the upper midwest and northeast U.S. demonstrated, the electric
transmission system serving the country has aged and is in need of
upgrade.&#160; The federal government
initiative to balance power supply and demand more effectively through the
multi-state regional transmission organizations is also creating a need for
permitting and engineering services to upgrade the transmission and
distribution grid.&#160; The Company&#146;s power
delivery engineering and environmental groups are assisting the owners of the
transmission grid with upgrading system capabilities to meet increases in
demand, improve reliability, and integrate new sources of electric generation.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Recognizing that
the needs of electrical users in high demand areas cannot be met solely by new
major generating plants or capital upgrades to the transmission grid, the
Company has also expanded its services into development and oversight of
distributed generation projects and energy capacity management consulting.&#160; The Company is currently co-developing a
number of distributed generation facilities located at host commercial office
and manufacturing operations.&#160; In
addition to maintaining a going-forward equity ownership position, the Company
provides licensing, engineering and development management services for the
facilities.&#160; This contribution of turnkey
services is aimed toward solving special customer problems, allowing the Company
to expand its ability to provide higher-value services.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s
financial transaction management group is assisting customers interested in
purchasing or selling energy assets.&#160;
These transactions are becoming increasingly more frequent because of
the benefits of having securitized assets and the need for capital in segments
of the energy industry.&#160; The Company&#146;s
services for these transactions include identifying available assets for
transfer as well as site-related due diligence and environmental services.&#160; These evolving activities continually allow
the Company to increase the overall value of its services.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.5pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='3',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Infrastructure
&#150; </font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s
infrastructure development markets are primarily targeted at: (1) the expanding
need for capacity in geographic areas where the population is growing rapidly;
and, (2) rehabilitative improvements of overburdened and deteriorating
infrastructure systems.&#160;&#160; Investing in
infrastructure projects continues to be a primary focus of government and industry
due to these drivers.&#160; The Company&#146;s
infrastructure market focus areas include:</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Transportation</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> - Planning, design and construction
management of road and bridge projects for public customers.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Land Development</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> - Planning, design and construction management
of development projects for municipal and private customers.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Water/Wastewater
Treatment</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> -
Planning, design and construction management for potable water and wastewater
treatment facilities.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Building Systems</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> - Building automation systems design,
central plant design, construction management and commissioning, and facility
energy management program implementation.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Security</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> - Vulnerability assessment, engineering
and structural improvements for public and private infrastructure facilities, and
design and implementation of security and surveillance systems.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Information Management</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> - Technology strategy and planning,
systems design and implementation for public and private sector customers.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-indent:-22.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Currently, much of
the Company&#146;s infrastructure work is accomplished through conventional
contracting.&#160; There is an increasing
trend for customers to prefer design/build or privatization (outsourcing)
contracts, and the Company is pursuing value-added contracting approaches.&#160; The Company&#146;s objective is to combine its
technical, financial, and risk management capabilities as a suite of
higher-margin, value-added services that enable the Company to capture larger
projects with the potential for greater profitability.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">At this time, it
is not practicable to report revenue by the environmental, energy/power and
infrastructure areas.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Customers"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Customers</font></b></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s customers include companies in the energy, chemical,
automotive, petroleum, construction, transportation, mining, waste management
and other industries, financial institutions, public utilities, and local,
state and federal government agencies.&#160;
Many of the Company&#146;s commercial customers are major multinational
corporations.&#160; The following are
representative of the Company&#146;s customers:</font></p>

  </td>
  <td width="238" valign="top" style="padding:0in 5.4pt 0in 5.4pt;width:178.8pt;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="257" valign="top" style="padding:0in 5.4pt 0in 5.4pt;width:192.6pt;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="239" valign="top" style="padding:0in 5.4pt 0in 5.4pt;width:179.4pt;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Unocal</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="238" valign="top" style="padding:0in 5.4pt 0in 5.4pt;width:178.8pt;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">For fiscal 2004, 2003 and 2002, agencies of the U.S. Government
(principally the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency and the U.S. Department
of Defense) accounted for 5%, 6% and 5%, respectively, of the Company&#146;s net
service revenue.&#160; No customer represented
10% or more of the Company&#146;s net service revenue in any of those years.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='4',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h1 align="left" style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><a name="MarketingAndSales"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Marketing and Sales</font></b></a></h1>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company believes that it attracts customers primarily on the basis
of its reputation for providing value-added and cost-effective solutions to
customer needs and its ability to respond to meet customer schedules.&#160; The marketing activities for the Company&#146;s
services are generally conducted by senior professional staff members and
executives (seller-doers) who are recognized experts in our business areas and
regularly meet with existing and potential customers to obtain new
business.&#160; These activities are typically
conducted through the Company&#146;s network of regional resource centers for local
customers and by market program leaders for national customers.&#160; In addition, corporate and subsidiary
marketing departments coordinate representation at trade shows, prepare sales
literature and develop and place advertising.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During the past three years a key element of the Company&#146;s marketing
and sales activities has been to focus on the highest level managers and
decision makers (e.g. CEOs, CFOs) of our customers.&#160; This approach has been successful in allowing
the Company to demonstrate its special value-added services to buyers who are
prepared to pay higher margins on larger projects when the Company can assist
them in accomplishing their most important objectives.&#160; This approach will be a continuing and
expanding strategy to assist the Company in achieving greater growth and higher
margins.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Backlog"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Backlog</font></b></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">At June&nbsp;30, 2004, the Company&#146;s net contract backlog (excluding
the estimated costs of pass-through charges) was approximately $250 million, as
compared to approximately $240 million at June&nbsp;30, 2003.&#160; The Company expects that approximately 60% of
this backlog will be completed in fiscal 2005.&#160;
In addition to this net contract backlog, the Company holds open order
contracts from various customers and government agencies.&#160; As work under these contracts is authorized
and funded, the Company includes this portion in its net contract backlog.&#160; While most contracts contain cancellation
provisions, the Company is unaware of any material work included in backlog
which will be canceled or delayed.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Employees"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Employees</font></b></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of June&nbsp;30, 2004, the Company had approximately 2,400 full- and
part-time employees.&#160; Approximately 85%
of these employees are engaged in performing environmental, power and
infrastructure engineering and consulting, financial, risk management,
construction management and information management services for customers.&#160; Many of these employees have master&#146;s degrees
or their equivalent and a number have Ph.D. degrees.&#160; The Company&#146;s professional staff includes
program managers, professional engineers and scientists, construction
specialists, computer programmers, systems analysts, attorneys and others with
degrees and experience that enable the Company to provide a diverse range of
services.&#160; The balance of the Company&#146;s
employees are engaged in executive, administrative and support activities.&#160; None of the Company&#146;s employees are
represented by a union.&#160; The Company
considers its relations with its employees to be very good.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Competition"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Competition</font></b></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The markets for many of the Company&#146;s services are highly
competitive.&#160; There are numerous
professional architectural, engineering and consulting firms and other
organizations which offer many of the services offered by the Company.&#160; The Company is subject to direct competition
with respect to the services it provides from many other firms, ranging from
small local firms to large national firms having substantially greater
financial, management and marketing resources than the Company. Competitive
factors include reputation, performance, price, geographic location and
availability of technically skilled personnel.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">However, the majority of the Company&#146;s work represents repeat orders
from long-term customers because the Company focuses on market areas where it
can be a leading provider due to staff skills, reputation, financial strength
and/or geographic presence.&#160; For example,
the Company believes that it is one of the top two or three providers of
licensing services for large energy projects.&#160;
Further, the Company believes that it is the market leader in providing
complete outsourcing of site remediation services through its Exit Strategy
program.&#160; In general, competition is
reduced when the Company can provide value-added, high-level problem-solving
services to senior management incorporating a combination of technical, risk
management and financial support services.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='5',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="GovernmentContracts"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Government
Contracts</font></b></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has contracts with agencies of
the U.S. Government and various state agencies which are subject to examination
and renegotiation.&#160; The Company believes
that adjustments resulting from such examination or renegotiation proceedings,
if any, will not have a material impact on the Company&#146;s operating results,
financial position or cash flows.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="RegulatoryMatters"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Regulatory
Matters</font></b></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s
businesses are subject to various rules and regulations at the federal, state
and local government levels.&#160; The Company
believes that it is in compliance with these rules and regulations.&#160; The Company has the appropriate licenses to
bid and perform work in the locations in which it operates.&#160; The Company has not experienced any
significant limitations on its business as a result of regulatory, bonding or
insurance requirements.&#160; The Company does
not believe any changes in law or changes in industry practice would limit
bidding on future projects.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Patents"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Patents</font></b></a><b><font style="font-weight:bold;">, Trademarks and Licenses</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has a number of trademarks, service marks, copyrights and
licenses, none of which are considered material to the Company&#146;s business as a
whole.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="EnvironmentalAndOtherConsiderations"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Environmental
and Other Considerations</font></b></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company does not believe that its compliance with federal, state
and local laws and regulations relating to the protection of the environment
will have any material effect on capital expenditures, earnings or competitive
position.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Item2"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Item 2</font></b></a><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Properties</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company provides its services through a network of over 85 offices
located nationwide.&#160; The Company leases
approximately 680,000 square feet of office and commercial space to support
these operations.&#160; In addition, a subsidiary
of the Company owns a 26,000 square foot office/warehouse building in Austin,
Texas.&#160; These properties are adequately
maintained and are suitable and adequate for the business activities conducted
therein.&#160; In connection with the
performance of certain Exit Strategy projects, affiliates of the Company have
taken title to sites on which those activities are being performed.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Item3"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item
3</font></b></a><b><font style="font-weight:bold;">.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Legal
Proceedings</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company and its subsidiaries are subject to claims and lawsuits
typical of those filed against engineering and consulting companies.&#160; The Company carries liability insurance,
including professional liability insurance, against such claims subject to
certain deductibles and policy limits.&#160;
Management is of the opinion that the resolution of these claims and
lawsuits (including the ones described immediately below) will not have a
material adverse effect on the Company&#146;s operating results, financial position
or cash flows.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">A subsidiary of the Company has been named as a defendant, along with a
number of other companies, in litigation in New Jersey Superior Court brought
on behalf of individuals claiming damages for alleged injuries related to a
collapse of several floors of a parking garage under construction in Atlantic
City, New Jersey.&#160; The litigation is in
early stages of discovery.&#160; The Company&#146;s
subsidiary had a limited inspection role in connection with the construction,
and management believes that it has meritorious defenses and is adequately
insured and that this matter will not have a material adverse effect on the
Company&#146;s operating results, financial position or cash flows.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">A purported predecessor of a subsidiary of the Company has been named
as one of over 50 defendants in a case filed in New York Supreme Court alleging
personal injuries to one individual related to potential historical exposure to
asbestos.&#160; To date there has been minimal
discovery in this case, and the Company has no reason to believe it has any
exposure.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Item4"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item
4</font></b></a><b><font style="font-weight:bold;">.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>Submission
of Matters to a Vote of Security Holders</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">None.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h2 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><a name="PartIi"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Part II</font></b></a></h2>

<h2 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h2>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><a name="Item5"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 5</font></b></a><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></b><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Market for Registrant&#146;s
Common Equity and Related Stockholder Matters</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
Company&#146;s common stock is traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the
symbol &#147;TRR&#148;.&#160;&#160; The following table sets
forth the high and low per share prices for the common stock for the fiscal
years ended June&nbsp;30, 2004 and 2003 as reported on the New York Stock
Exchange:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On
September&nbsp;9, 2004, there were approximately 3,500 holders of the Company&#146;s
common stock, of which 289 were shareholders of record.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">To
date the Company has not paid any cash dividends on its common stock.&#160; The payment of dividends in the future will
be subject to the financial condition, capital requirements and earnings of the
Company.&#160; However, future earnings are
expected to be used for expansion of the Company&#146;s operations, and cash
dividends are not currently anticipated.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Item7"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 7</font></b></a><b><font style="font-weight:bold;">.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></b><b><font style="font-weight:bold;">Management&#146;s Discussion and
Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Management&#146;s
Discussion and Analysis contains statements that are forward-looking.&#160; These statements are based upon current
expectations and assumptions that are subject to risks and uncertainties.&#160; Actual results could differ materially due to
a number of factors.&#160; See the discussion
in &#147;Forward-Looking Statements&#148; on page 16.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h1 align="left" style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Critical Accounting Policies</font></b></h1>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s financial statements have been prepared in accordance
with accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of
America.&#160; These principles require the
use of estimates and assumptions that affect amounts reported and disclosed in
the financial statements and related notes.&#160;
Actual results could differ from these estimates and assumptions.&#160; Management uses its best judgment in the
assumptions used to value these estimates, which are based on current facts and
circumstances, prior experience and other assumptions that are believed to be
reasonable.&#160; The Company&#146;s accounting
policies are described in Note 2 of the Notes to Consolidated Financial
Statements contained in Item 8 of this Annual Report on Form 10-K.&#160; Management believes the following critical
accounting policies reflect the more significant judgments and estimates used
in preparation of the Company&#146;s consolidated financial statements and are the
policies which are most critical in the portrayal of the Company&#146;s financial
position and results of operations:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Long-term contracts:</font></i></b>&#160; The Company recognizes contract
revenue in accordance with American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
Statement of Position 81-1, &#147;Accounting for Performance of Construction-Type
and Certain Production-Type Contracts&#148;.&#160; The Company records revenue on its cost-type
and time and material contracts based upon direct labor costs and other direct
contract costs incurred. The
Company has fixed price Exit Strategy contracts to remediate environmental
conditions at contaminated sites under which the Company and its customers are
protected against cost overruns by insurance policies.&#160; Revenue (and related costs) from these fixed
price contracts is recognized using the efforts-expended,
percentage-of-completion method of accounting based on total costs incurred to
date as compared to total costs estimated at completion.&#160; Contract costs include direct labor costs,
subcontractor costs, other direct costs and indirect costs.&#160; The Company&#146;s method of revenue recognition
requires the Company to prepare estimates of costs to complete for contracts in
progress.&#160; In making such estimates,
judgments are required to evaluate contingencies, such as potential variances
in schedule&nbsp;and labor and other contract costs, liability claims, contract
disputes or achievement of contractual performance standards.&#160; Changes in total estimated contract costs and
losses, if any, are recognized in the period they are determined.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Allowances for doubtful accounts:</font></i></b>&#160; Allowances for doubtful accounts are
maintained for estimated potential losses resulting from the failure of our
customers to make required payments.&#160;
Allowances for doubtful accounts have been determined through reviews of
specific amounts deemed to be uncollectible, potential write-offs as a result
of customers who have filed for bankruptcy protection, plus an allowance for
other amounts for which some potential loss is determined to be probable based
on current circumstances.&#160; If the
financial condition of customers were to change, or if the Company's assessment
as to collectibility were to change, adjustments to the allowances may be
required.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Income
taxes:</font></i></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; At June&nbsp;30, 2004, the Company had
approximately $6.2 million of deferred income tax benefits.&#160; The realization of a portion of these
benefits is dependent on the Company&#146;s estimates of future taxable income and
its tax planning strategies.&#160; A $0.5
million valuation allowance has been recorded in relation to certain state loss
carryforwards, which will more likely than not expire unused.&#160; Management believes that sufficient taxable
income will be earned in the future to realize the remaining deferred income
tax benefits. However, the realization of these deferred income tax benefits
can be impacted by changes to tax codes, statutory tax rates and future taxable
income levels.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Business acquisitions:</font></i></b>&#160; Assets and liabilities acquired in business
combinations are recorded at their estimated fair values at the acquisition
date.&#160; At June&nbsp;30, 2004, the Company
had approximately $111.8 million of goodwill, representing the cost of
acquisitions in excess of fair values assigned to the underlying net assets of
acquired</p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">9</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='9',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">companies.&#160; In accordance with
Statement of Financial Accounting Standards (SFAS) No. 142, &#147;Goodwill and Other
Intangible Assets&#148;, goodwill and intangible assets deemed to have indefinite
lives are not amortized, but are subject to annual impairment testing.&#160; The assessment of goodwill involves the
estimation of the fair value of the Company&#146;s reporting unit, as defined by
SFAS No. 142.&#160; Management completed this
assessment during the third quarter of fiscal 2004, based on the best
information available as of the date of assessment, and determined that no
impairment existed.&#160; There can be no
assurance that future events will not result in impairments of goodwill or
other assets.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Results
of Operations</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In the course of providing its services, the
Company routinely subcontracts drilling, laboratory analyses, construction
equipment and other services.&#160; These
costs are passed directly through to customers and, in accordance with industry
practice, are included in Gross Revenue.&#160;
Because subcontractor costs and direct charges can vary significantly
from project to project, the Company considers NSR, which is gross revenue less
subcontractor costs and direct charges, as its primary measure of revenue
growth.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <td width="57%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:57.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Dividends and accretion charges on
  preferred stock</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="12%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">0.3</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="12%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.0%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">0.3</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>

  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">0.2</font></p>

  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.0%;">

  <td width="57%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:57.0%;">
