<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">2004 Compared to 2003</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
revenue growth trend established in fiscal 1998 continued for the seventh
consecutive year.&#160; Gross revenue
increased $53.2 million, or 16.9%, to $368.8 million in fiscal 2004, from
$315.6 million in fiscal 2003.&#160; Organic
activities (existing business) provided $37.0 million or 70% of gross revenue
growth in fiscal 2004, with acquisitions providing the remaining $16.2 million
or 30% of the gross revenue growth.&#160; The
increase from organic activities relates principally to significant
subcontractor efforts incurred on certain Exit Strategy and other construction
contracts during fiscal 2004.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NSR increased $16.3 million, or 7.5%, to
$233.6 million in fiscal 2004, from $217.3 million in fiscal 2003.&#160; Acquisitions provided $12.7 million or 78% of
NSR growth in fiscal 2004, with organic activities providing the remaining $3.6
million or 22% of the NSR growth. Organic activities continued to be affected
by challenging economic conditions, particularly for large capital projects
which generally lag behind initial periods of economic upturns, although in the
fourth quarter organic activities provided 94% of the NSR growth.&#160; Going forward, organic growth is expected to
increase to support management&#146;s goal of providing a</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">10</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='10',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">reasonable balance between organic and
acquisition growth over a several year period.&#160;
NSR from acquired companies is considered part of acquisition growth
during the twelve months following the date acquired.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cost of services increased $11.1 million, or
6.0%, to $195.6 million in fiscal 2004, from $184.5 million in fiscal
2003.&#160; The increase was primarily from
the increase in NSR.&#160; As a percentage of
NSR, cost of services decreased to 83.8% in fiscal 2004 from 84.9% in fiscal
2003.&#160; The improvement over last year
resulted from cost reductions made to align capacity with the pace of activity.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">General and administrative expenses increased
$2.1 million, or 32.2%, to $8.6 million in fiscal 2004, from $6.5 million in
fiscal 2003.&#160; These increases were
primarily due to additional costs required to support the Company&#146;s growth,
costs associated with the Company&#146;s initial efforts to become compliant with
the requirements of Section&nbsp;404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 and an
additional $0.5 million of costs necessary to complete the Company&#146;s fiscal
2003 audit.&#160; The additional audit costs
were recorded in the first quarter of fiscal 2004 and relate to the Company&#146;s
change in accounting for long-term fixed price contracts in fiscal 2003.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Depreciation and amortization increased $0.6
million, or 11.8%, to $5.7 million in fiscal 2004, from $5.1 million in fiscal
2003.&#160; This increase was primarily
attributable to the additional depreciation expense related to capital
expenditures completed in fiscal 2004 and 2003, as well as the additional
depreciation expense associated with acquisitions completed in fiscal 2003.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Interest
expense increased by approximately 3.5% in fiscal 2004, as compared to fiscal
2003, primarily due to higher average borrowings outstanding against the
Company&#146;s revolving credit facility to finance acquisition payments. The
Company&#146;s percentage of debt to capitalization ratio was reduced by
approximately 2.9% to 19.8% in fiscal 2004.&#160;
This ratio continues to remain relatively low, reflecting management&#146;s
conservative debt philosophy.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As a result of the above factors, income from
operations increased by $2.5 million, or 11.8%, to $23.7 million in fiscal
2004, from $21.2 million in fiscal 2003.&#160;
For fiscal 2004, acquisitions provided $1.9 million or 75% of the growth
in operating income, and organic activities provided the remaining $0.6 million
or 25% of the fiscal 2004 growth.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
provisions for federal and state income taxes reflect effective rates of 40.8%
in fiscal 2004 compared to 38.5% in fiscal 2003.&#160; These increases were primarily due to the
distribution of revenue in states with higher state income taxes.&#160; The Company believes that there will be
sufficient taxable income in future periods to enable utilization of available
deferred income tax benefits.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">2003 Compared to 2002</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
revenue growth trend established in fiscal 1998 continued for the sixth
consecutive year.&#160; Gross revenue
increased $46.1 million, or 17.1%, to $315.6 million in fiscal 2003, from
$269.5 million in fiscal 2002.&#160; NSR
increased $37.3 million, or 20.7%, to $217.3 million in fiscal 2003, from
$180.1 million in fiscal 2002.&#160; The
fiscal 2003 NSR growth was entirely attributable to acquisitions. Approximately
104% of NSR growth for fiscal 2003 was from acquisitions while organic
(existing business) NSR decreased by 4% compared to the prior year.&#160; Management&#146;s goal continues to be to provide
a reasonable balance between organic and acquisition growth over a several year
period.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Income from operations decreased by $5.0
million to $21.2 million in fiscal 2003 compared to $26.2 million in fiscal
2002.&#160; Acquisitions provided
approximately $5.1 million of operating income growth, while operating income
from organic activities decreased by approximately $10.1 million. Management&#146;s
objective is to also maintain a reasonable balance over time between increases
in income from operations derived from organic and acquisition growth.&#160; For example, for fiscal years 2000 through
2002, income from operations increases from organic and acquisition growth were
52% and 48%, respectively.&#160; The inability
to achieve this balance in fiscal 2003 (as well as the increase in cost of
services as a percentage of NSR) was due to a reduction in the utilization of
the Company&#146;s staff related to three primary factors as follows:</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">challenging economic conditions which affected several of the Company&#146;s
operating centers.&#160; Until fiscal 2003,
these types of market changes were mitigated by increases in higher-margin
business.&#160; The area most affected was the
transportation infrastructure market where the Company relies on funding from
state and local government entities;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">11</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='11',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the delay in new contract awards for Exit Strategy and other
higher-margin programs attributable to economic conditions, general customer
uncertainty regarding capital investments, and unusual winter conditions; and</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">costs associated with substantial hiring and project investments the Company
made in key markets which are expected to grow in spite of current economic
conditions.&#160; Examples include: the
planning, design and installation of electric power transmission and
distribution systems and the installation of distributed generation power
systems; assisting customers in the planning and design of infrastructure
security systems; and assisting customers in evaluating financial aspects of
acquiring or divesting energy related assets.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-align:left;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In fiscal 2003,
management implemented actions to reduce costs, increase operational
efficiencies and align capacity with the current pace of activity.&#160; As these actions continue to yield results,
and recent investments increasingly yield positive gains, management expects
that a closer balance between organic and acquisition growth will again be
realized.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">General and administrative expenses (G&amp;A)
increased approximately 26.0% in fiscal 2003 primarily due to additional costs
necessary to support the Company&#146;s growth as well as increased costs for audit
and legal services.&#160; However, as a
percentage of NSR, G&amp;A expenses only increased to 3.0% in fiscal 2003 from
2.8% in fiscal 2002.&#160; This demonstrates
the Company&#146;s ability to generate and manage the increased revenue without
adding a proportional amount of overhead, resulting in higher margins for all
services performed.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Depreciation and amortization expense
increased approximately 48.7% in fiscal 2003.&#160;
This increase was primarily attributable to the additional depreciation
expense of capital expenditures made throughout the year, the amortization
expense related to identifiable intangibles from acquisitions, as well as the
additional depreciation expense associated with acquisitions completed in
fiscal 2003 and 2002.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Interest
expense increased by approximately 23.2% in fiscal 2003 compared to last year
primarily due to higher average borrowings outstanding against the Company&#146;s
revolving credit facility to finance acquisitions. The Company&#146;s percentage of
debt to capitalization ratio of 22.7% continues to remain relatively low,
reflecting management&#146;s conservative debt philosophy.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
provision for federal and state income taxes reflected an effective rate of
38.5% in fiscal 2003 compared to 38.3% in fiscal 2002.&#160; This small increase was primarily due to
higher state income taxes.&#160; The Company
believes that there will be sufficient taxable income in future periods to
enable utilization of available deferred income tax benefits.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
Company changed its method of accounting for fixed price contracts, effective
July&nbsp;1, 2002.&#160; The cumulative effect
of the change decreased net income by $2.4 million (net of income taxes of $1.5
million) or $0.17 per diluted share, which was retroactively reflected in the
first quarter of fiscal 2003.&#160; Before the
cumulative effect adjustment, this accounting change had the effect of
increasing revenue, net income and diluted earnings per share in fiscal 2003 by
$2.5 million, $1.5 million and $0.11, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Impact
of Inflation</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
Company&#146;s operations have not been materially affected by inflation or changing
prices because of the short-term nature of many of its contracts and the fact
that most contracts of a longer term are subject to adjustment or have been
priced to cover anticipated increases in labor and other costs.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Liquidity
and Capital Resources</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company
primarily relies on cash from operations and financing activities, including
borrowings based upon the strength of its balance sheet, to fund
operations.&#160; The Company&#146;s liquidity is
assessed in terms of its overall ability to generate cash to fund its operating
and investing activities and to reduce debt.&#160;
Of particular importance in the management of liquidity are cash flows
generated from operating activities, acquisitions, capital expenditure levels
and an adequate bank line of credit.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash flows from
operating activities were $11.5 million in fiscal 2004, compared to $12.1
million last year.&#160; This type of
year-to-year variance is typical primarily due to the timing of the collection
of accounts receivable.&#160; During fiscal
2004, much of the cash was provided by: (1) the $13.2 million of net income;
(2) the $5.7 million charge for depreciation and amortization expense; (3) the
$3.1 million provision for doubtful accounts; and (4) the $3.0</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">12</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.5pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='12',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">million increase
in accrued compensation and benefits due to the timing of payroll
payments.&#160; The cash was offset by the
$19.3 million increase in accounts receivable resulting from the Company&#146;s
revenue growth.&#160; The increase in net
accounts receivable in fiscal 2004 was 17%, which is equal to the percentage
increase in gross revenue for the year.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Accounts receivable include both: (1) billed receivables associated
with invoices submitted for work previously completed and (2) unbilled
receivables (work in progress).&#160; The
unbilled receivables are primarily related to work performed in the last month
of the fiscal year.&#160; The magnitude of
accounts receivable for a professional services company is typically evaluated
as days sales outstanding (DSO), which is calculated by dividing both current
and long-term receivables by the most recent six-month average of daily gross
revenue. Despite the increase in accounts receivable due to the growth in gross
revenue, DSO, which measures the collections turnover of both billed and
unbilled receivables, has remained constant at 117 year over year.&#160; Management&#146;s long-term goal is to reduce DSO
to 100 &#150; 105 days.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Billings in advance of revenue earned represents amounts collected in
accordance with contractual terms before the work is performed, primarily on
Exit Strategy contracts.&#160; These payments
provide the Company with an advance source of cash for one to several
years.&#160; During years when the amount of
payments from Exit Strategy contracts exceeds the amount of revenue recognized
as work is performed on the contracts, billings in advance of revenue earned
will increase and thereby provide operating cash flows.&#160; During fiscal 2004, the increase in billings
in advance of revenue earned contributed $1.2 million towards the total cash
provided by operating activities.&#160; During
years when the amount of revenue recognized from work performed exceeds the
amount of payments from Exit Strategy contracts, billings in advance of revenue
earned will decrease and reduce operating cash flows.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Upon signing of each contract with a finite cost cap insurance policy,
a majority of the contract price is directly deposited in a restricted account
held by American International Group (AIG).&#160;
AIG then pays the Company from the deposited funds as work is
performed.&#160; The long-term insurance
receivable is associated with amounts held by AIG for work performed, but which
are currently not yet payable under the terms of the policy.&#160; These amounts will be paid as the Company
completes work on the project.&#160; The
effect on periodic operating cash flows will vary depending upon the mix of
work performed, but not yet payable and amounts reimbursed to the Company by
AIG.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Investing activities used cash of approximately $12.3 million in fiscal
2004 compared to $25.6 million last year.&#160;
The decrease was primarily due to the Company&#146;s planned, temporary
reduction in acquisitions during fiscal 2004.&#160;
The investments this year consisted of: (1) $5.7 million for
acquisitions (approximately $0.6 million for current year acquisitions and
approximately $5.1 million for additional purchase price payments related to
acquisitions completed in prior years), (2) approximately $4.7 million for
capital expenditures for additional information technology and other equipment
to support business growth and (3) an additional $2.1 million for investments
in unconsolidated affiliates.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During
fiscal 2004, the Company&#146;s financing activities used cash of $0.8 million.&#160; This cash, which was primarily provided by
the exercise of stock options and warrants, was used to repay long-term debt.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
Company maintains a banking arrangement with Wachovia Bank, National
Association in syndication with three additional banks that provides a
revolving credit facility to support operating and investing activities.&#160; In March&nbsp;2004, the agreement was amended
and restated with the amount available pursuant to the credit facility
increased to $60 million from $50 million and the term extended to
March&nbsp;2007.&#160; Borrowings under the
agreement bear interest at Wachovia&#146;s base rate or the Eurodollar rate plus or
minus applicable margins and would be due and payable in March&nbsp;2007 when
the agreement expires, unless it is extended as management expects.&#160; Borrowings under the agreement are collateralized
by accounts receivable.&#160; At June&nbsp;30,
2004 outstanding borrowings pursuant to the agreement were $40.5 million, at an
average interest rate of 2.9%, compared to $41.0 million of outstanding
borrowings, at an average interest rate of 2.9% at June&nbsp;30, 2003.&#160; During fiscal 2004, the Company significantly
increased the free availability under the revolving credit facility.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
credit facility provides for affirmative and negative covenants that limit,
among other things, the Company&#146;s ability to incur other indebtedness, dispose
of assets, pay cash dividends on its common stock and make certain
investments.&#160; In addition, the agreement
includes certain restrictive covenants, including, but not limited to, the
minimum ratio of adjusted EBITDA to interest expense, income taxes and the
current portion of long-term debt, and the minimum ratio of adjusted current
assets to adjusted total liabilities.&#160;
The Company was in compliance with all covenants as of June&nbsp;30, 2004.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">13</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='13',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Management
expects that the cash generated from operations, the cash on hand at
June&nbsp;30, 2004 and available borrowings under the revolving credit facility
will be sufficient to meet the Company&#146;s cash requirements for currently
anticipated activities.&#160; If the Company
pursues material acquisitions or investments in the future in which the
potential cash consideration approaches or exceeds the availability of current
sources, the Company would either increase its lending facility or pursue
additional financing.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Contractual

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Off-Balance
Sheet Arrangements</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
Company does not have any Off-Balance Sheet Arrangements other than operating
leases for most of its office facilities and certain equipment.&#160; Rental expense for operating leases was
approximately $12.3 million in fiscal 2004; $11.2 million in fiscal 2003; and $8.1
million in fiscal 2002.&#160; Minimum
operating lease obligations payable in future years (i.e., primarily base rent)
are presented above in Contractual Obligations.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">New Accounting Guidance</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="color:black;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" color="black" face="Times New Roman" style="color:windowtext;font-size:10.0pt;">In
November&nbsp;2002, the Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) issued
Interpretation 45, &#147;Guarantor&#146;s Accounting and Disclosure Requirements for
Guarantees, Including Indirect Guarantees of Indebtedness of Others&#148; (FIN
45).&#160; FIN 45 clarifies the existing
disclosure requirements for most guarantees, including loan guarantees such as
standby letters of credit.&#160; It also
clarifies that at the time a company issues a guarantee, the company must
recognize an initial liability for the fair value, or market value, of the
obligations it assumes under the guarantee and must disclose that information
in its interim and annual financial statements.&#160;
The initial recognition and initial measurement provisions apply on a
prospective basis to guarantees issued or modified after December&nbsp;31,
2002.&#160; The adoption of this
interpretation did not have a material impact on the Company&#146;s financial
statements.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="color:black;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" color="black" face="Times New Roman" style="color:windowtext;font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="color:black;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" color="black" face="Times New Roman" style="color:windowtext;font-size:10.0pt;">In
November&nbsp;2002, the Emerging Issues Task Force (EITF) of the FASB reached a
consensus on Issue No. 00-21, &#147;Multiple-Deliverable Revenue Arrangements&#148;.&#160; EITF No. 00-21 addresses how to account for
revenue arrangements with multiple deliverables and provides guidance relating
to when such arrangements should be divided into components for revenue
recognition purposes.&#160; The consensus is
effective for revenue agreements entered into in fiscal periods beginning after
June&nbsp;15, 2003 with early adoption permitted.&#160; The Company adopted the provisions of EITF
No. 00-21 in the quarter ended September&nbsp;30, 2003, and the effect of
adoption was immaterial.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="color:black;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" color="black" face="Times New Roman" style="color:windowtext;font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In January&nbsp;2003, the FASB released
Interpretation No. 46, &#147;Consolidation of Variable Interest Entities&#148; (FIN 46),
which requires that all primary beneficiaries of Variable Interest Entities
(VIE) consolidate those entities.&#160; FIN 46
is effective immediately for VIEs created after January&nbsp;31, 2003 and to
VIEs in which an enterprise obtains an interest after that date.&#160; It applies in the first fiscal year or
interim period beginning after June&nbsp;15, 2003 to VIEs in which an
enterprise holds a variable interest it acquired before February&nbsp;1,
2003.&#160; In December&nbsp;2003, the FASB
published a revision to FIN 46 (FIN 46R) to clarify some of the provisions of
the interpretation and to defer the effective date of implementation for
certain entities.&#160; Under the guidance of
FIN 46R, entities that do not have interests in structures that are commonly referred
to as special purpose entities are required to apply the provisions of the
interpretation in financial statements for periods ending after March&nbsp;14,
2004.&#160; The Company does not have
interests in special purpose entities.&#160;
The Company adopted the provisions of FIN 46R in the quarter ended
March&nbsp;31, 2004, and the effect of adoption was immaterial.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">14</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='14',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In April&nbsp;2003, the FASB issued SFAS
No.&nbsp;149, &#147;Amendment of Statement 133 on Derivative Instruments and Hedging
Activities&#148;, which amends and clarifies financial accounting and reporting for
derivative instruments, including certain derivative instruments embedded in
other contracts and for hedging activities under SFAS No.&nbsp;133, &#147;Accounting
for Derivative Instruments and Hedging Activities&#148;.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;149, which is to be applied
prospectively, is effective for contracts entered into or modified after
June&nbsp;30, 2003, and for hedging relationships designated after
June&nbsp;30, 2003.&#160; The Company adopted
the provisions of SFAS No. 149 in the quarter ended March&nbsp;31, 2004, and
the effect of adoption was immaterial.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In May&nbsp;2003, the FASB issued SFAS
No.&nbsp;150, &#147;Accounting for Certain Financial Instruments with
Characteristics of Both Liabilities and Equity&#148;, which establishes standards
for how an issuer classifies and measures certain financial instruments with
characteristics of both liabilities and equity.&#160;
It requires that an issuer classify a financial instrument that is
within its scope as a liability (or an asset in some circumstances), and is
effective for instruments entered into or modified after May&nbsp;31, 2003 and
otherwise is effective at the beginning of the first interim period beginning
after June&nbsp;15, 2003.&#160; The provisions
regarding guidance for accounting for mandatorily redeemable non-controlling
interests in subsidiaries that would not be liabilities under SFAS No. 150 has
been deferred for an indefinite period.&#160;
The adoption of the currently effective provisions of this standard did
not have a material impact on the Company&#146;s results of operations or financial
position.&#160; The Company has specifically
reviewed whether its redeemable preferred stock is within the scope of this
standard, and, due to the redemption value being subject to a floor share
price, the monetary amount known at inception was not deemed to be fixed.&nbsp;
As a result, the Company&#146;s redeemable preferred stock does not fall within the
scope of this standard.&#160; The Company is
currently reviewing the deferred provisions and assessing their impact.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2004, the EITF reached a
consensus regarding Issue No. 03-16, &#147;Accounting for Investments in Limited
Liability Companies&#148;.&#160; EITF 03-16
requires investments in limited liability companies that have separate
ownership accounts for each investor to be accounted for similarly to a limited
partnership investment under Statement of Position No. 78-9, &#147;Accounting for
Investments in Real Estate Ventures&#148;.&#160;
Investors would be required to apply the equity method of accounting to
their investments at a much lower ownership threshold than the 20% threshold
applied under Accounting Principles Board Opinion No. 18, &#147;The Equity Method of
Accounting for Investments in Common Stock&#148;.&#160;
EITF 03-16 is effective for the first period beginning after
June&nbsp;15, 2004 and will be applied as a change in accounting principle with
a cumulative effect reflected in the income statement.&#160; The new accounting guidance in EITF 03-16
requires the Company to change its method of accounting for its 19.9%
investment in EnergySolve, LLC (EnergySolve) from the cost method to the equity
method effective July&nbsp;1, 2004.&#160;&#160;&#160;
Accordingly, the Company will record an adjustment for the cumulative
effect of this change in accounting principle equal to its pro rata share of
EnergySolve&#146;s losses since making its investment in this start-up energy
savings company in fiscal 2001.&nbsp; The Company estimates that the adjustment
will&nbsp;decrease net income&nbsp;by&nbsp;approximately&nbsp;$0.6 million (net
of income taxes of $0.4 million) or $0.04 per diluted share in the
first&nbsp;quarter of&nbsp;fiscal 2005.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In April&nbsp;2004, the FASB issued FASB
Staff Position (FSP) No. 129-1, &#147;Disclosure Requirements under FASB Statement
No. 129, Disclosure of Information about Capital Structure, and Relating to
Contingently Convertible Securities&#148;.&#160;
The FASB staff confirmed through this FSP that the disclosure
requirements of FASB Statement No. 129 apply to all contingently convertible
financial instruments, including those containing contingent conversion
requirements that have not been met and are not otherwise required to be
included in the computation of diluted earnings per share.&#160; The Company has included these required
disclosures in Note 9 of the Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements
contained in Item 8 of this Annual Report on Form 10-K.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">15</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='15',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Forward-Looking
Statements</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Certain
statements in this report may be forward-looking statements within the meaning
of Section&nbsp;27A of the Securities Act of 1933 and Section&nbsp;21E of the
Securities Exchange Act of 1934.&#160; Such
statements are based on management&#146;s current estimates, expectations and
projections about the factors discussed.&#160;
By their nature, such forward-looking statements involve risks and
uncertainties.&#160; The Company has attempted
to identify such statements using words such as &#147;may&#148;, &#147;expects&#148;, &#147;plans&#148;,
&#147;anticipates&#148;, &#147;believes&#148;, &#147;estimates&#148;, or other words of similar import.&#160; The Company cautions the reader that there
may be events in the future that management is not able to accurately predict
or control which may cause actual results to differ materially from the
expectations described in the forward-looking statements, including the
following examples:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>increase in competition by foreign and
domestic competitors;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>availability of qualified engineers and other
professional staff needed to perform contracts;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>continuation of income from core activities
to finance growth;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>availability of environmental insurance for
certain Exit Strategy projects;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>the timing of new awards and the funding of
such awards;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>the ability of the Company to meet project
performance or schedule&nbsp;requirements;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>continuation of regulatory enforcement
requirements for a portion of the Company&#146;s services;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>cost overruns on fixed, maximum or unit
priced contracts;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>the outcome of pending and future litigation
and government proceedings;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>the cyclical nature of the individual markets
in which the Company&#146;s customers operate;</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>the successful closing and/or subsequent
integration of any merger or acquisition transaction; and,</p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">&#149;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>the ability of the Company to raise capital
in the debt or equity markets.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The preceding list is not all-inclusive, and
the Company undertakes no obligation to publicly update any forward-looking
statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h1 align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><a name="Item7a"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Item
7A</font></b></a><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Quantitative
and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk</font></h1>

<h1 align="left" style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h1>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
Company&#146;s exposure to market risk for changes in interest rates relates
primarily to borrowings under the Company&#146;s revolving credit agreement.&#160; These borrowings bear interest at variable
rates and the fair value of this indebtedness is not significantly affected by
changes in market interest rates.&#160; An
effective increase or decrease of 10% in the current effective interest rate
under the revolving credit facility would not have a material effect on the
Company&#146;s operating results, financial condition or cash flows.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h1 align="left" style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><a name="Item8"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Item 8</font></b></a><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">.</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Financial Statements and Supplementary Data</font></h1>

<h1 align="left" style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h1>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="ReportOfIndependentRegisteredPublic"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Report of Independent
Registered Public Accounting Firm</font></b></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">To the
Shareholders and Board of Directors of:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">TRC Companies,
Inc.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Windsor,
Connecticut</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have
audited the accompanying consolidated balance sheets of TRC Companies, Inc. and
subsidiaries (the Company) as of June&nbsp;30, 2004 and 2003, and the related
consolidated statements of income, changes in shareholders&#146; equity and cash
flows for the years then ended. Our audits also included the financial
statement schedules for the years ended June&nbsp;30, 2004 and 2003 listed in
the Index at 15 (a)(2).&#160; These consolidated
financial statements and financial statement schedules are the responsibility
of the Company&#146;s management. Our responsibility is to express an opinion on
these consolidated financial statements and financial statement schedules based
on our audits.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We conducted
our audits in accordance with standards of the Public Company Accounting
Oversight Board (United States). Those standards require that we plan and
perform the audit to obtain reasonable assurance about whether the consolidated
financial statements are free of material misstatement. An audit includes
examining, on a test basis, evidence supporting the amounts and disclosures in
the financial statements. An audit also includes assessing the accounting
principles used and significant estimates made by management, as well as
evaluating the overall financial statement presentation. We believe that our
audits provide a reasonable basis for our opinion.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In our
opinion, such consolidated financial statements present fairly, in all material
respects, the financial position of TRC Companies, Inc. and subsidiaries as of
June&nbsp;30, 2004 and 2003, and the results of their operations and their cash
flows for the years then ended, in conformity with accounting principles
generally accepted in the United States of America.&#160; Also, in our opinion, such 2004 and 2003
financial statement schedules, when considered in relation to the basic 2004
and 2003 consolidated financial statements taken as a whole, present fairly in
all material respects the information set forth therein.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As discussed
in Note 2 to the consolidated financial statements, the Company changed its
method for measuring progress under the percentage of completion method of
accounting for long-term fixed price contracts effective July&nbsp;1, 2002.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="Report"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Report </font></b></a><b><font style="font-weight:bold;">of Independent Registered Public
Accounting Firm</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">To the
Shareholders and Board of Directors of TRC Companies, Inc.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In our opinion,
the consolidated statements of income, cash flows, and changes in shareholders&#146;
equity for the year ended June&nbsp;30, 2002 presents fairly, in all material
respects, the results of operations and cash flows of TRC Companies, Inc. and
its subsidiaries for the year ended June&nbsp;30, 2002 in conformity with
accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America.&#160; In addition, in our opinion, the financial
statement schedule&nbsp;listed in the accompanying index presents fairly, in
all material respects, the information set forth therein for the year ended
June&nbsp;30, 2002 when read in conjunction with the related consolidated
financial statements.&#160; These financial
statements and financial statement schedule&nbsp;are the responsibility of the
Company&#146;s management.&#160; Our responsibility
is to express an opinion on these financial statements and financial statement
schedule&nbsp;based on our audit.&#160; We
conducted our audit of these statements in accordance with the standards of the
Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (United States).&#160; Those standards require that we plan and
perform the audit to obtain reasonable assurance about whether the financial
statements are free of material misstatement.&#160;
An audit includes examining, on a test basis, evidence supporting the
amounts and disclosures in the financial statements, assessing the accounting
principles used and significant estimates made by management, and evaluating
the overall financial statement presentation.&#160;
We believe that our audit provides a reasonable basis for our opinion.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As discussed
in Notes 6 and 7, effective July&nbsp;1, 2001, the Company adopted the
provisions of Statement of Financial Accounting Standards (SFAS) No. 141,
&#147;Business Combinations&#148;, and SFAS No. 142, &#147;Goodwill and Other Intangible
Assets&#148;.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <td width="6%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border-bottom:double windowtext 1.5pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:6.08%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:7.0pt;">(2,897</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.36%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:7.0pt;">)</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:double windowtext 1.5pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.3%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:7.0pt;">$</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="6%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border-bottom:double windowtext 1.5pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:6.74%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:7.0pt;">157,797</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="0%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:.96%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

</table>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt -.4pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">See accompanying notes to consolidated
financial statements.</font></i></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">22</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><a name="NotesToConsolidatedFinancialStatemen"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">NOTES TO
CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS</font></b></a></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:normal;">In thousands,
except per share data</font></i></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Note 1.&#160;
</font></b><b><font style="font-weight:bold;">Company Background and Basis of Presentation</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">TRC Companies, Inc. (the Company) is a
customer-focused company that creates and implements sophisticated and
innovative solutions to the challenges facing America&#146;s environmental, energy,
infrastructure and security markets.&#160; The
Company conducts it activities under one business segment which involves
providing engineering and consulting services to commercial organizations and
governmental agencies primarily in the United States of America.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
consolidated financial statements include the Company and its wholly owned
subsidiaries, after elimination of intercompany accounts and transactions.&#160; Investments in which the Company has the
ability to exercise significant influence, but not control, are accounted for
by the equity method.&#160; Investments in
which the Company does not have the ability to exercise significant influence
are accounted for by the cost method.&#160;
Certain contracts are executed jointly through alliances and joint
ventures with unrelated third parties. The Company recognizes its proportional
share of such joint venture revenue, costs and operating profits in its
Consolidated Statements of Income.&#160;
Certain financial statement items have been reclassified to conform to
the current year&#146;s presentation.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Note 2.&#160;
</font></b><b><font style="font-weight:bold;">Summary of </font>Significant Accounting Policies</b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Revenue Recognition</font></i></b><b><i><font style="font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;"> &#150; </font></i></b>The Company recognizes contract revenue in
accordance with American Institute of Certified Public Accountants Statement of
Position (SOP) 81-1, &#147;Accounting for Performance of Construction-Type and
Certain Production-Type Contracts&#148;.&#160; The
Company records revenue on its cost-type and time and material contracts based
upon direct labor costs and other direct contract costs incurred. The Company
has fixed price Exit Strategy contracts to remediate environmental conditions
at contaminated sites.&#160; Revenue (and
related costs) from these contracts is recognized using the efforts-expended,
percentage-of-completion method of accounting based on total costs incurred to
date as compared to total costs estimated at completion.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Certain Exit Strategy contracts are segmented into two profit centers:
(1) remediation and (2) operation, maintenance and monitoring pursuant to SOP
81-1. Costs and revenue on long-term contracts are subject to revision
throughout the lives of the contracts, and any required adjustments are made in
the period in which the revisions become known.&#160;
Losses on contracts are recorded in the period in which they are
identified.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On contracts where there are billings in advance of revenue earned, the
excess is presented on the balance sheet as a current liability.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Change in Accounting for Revenue
Recognition &#150; </font></i></b>In
the fourth quarter of fiscal 2003, the Company changed certain elements of its
application of the percentage-of-completion method of accounting for fixed
price contracts, effective July&nbsp;1, 2002.&#160;
Beginning in fiscal 2003, the Company measures progress towards
completion based on total contract costs, including direct labor costs,
subcontractor costs, other direct costs and indirect costs.&#160; Prior to fiscal 2003, the Company measured
progress towards completion based on direct labor costs, but for contracts
where subcontractor costs resulted in a more meaningful measure of progress
towards completion, they were considered for inclusion with labor costs.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In the Company&#146;s opinion, the new method more
clearly reflects the Company&#146;s measure of progress toward completion of its
fixed price contracts.&#160; The cumulative
effect of the accounting change decreased net income by $2,361 (net of income
taxes of $1,478) or $0.17 per diluted share, which was retroactively reflected
in the first quarter of fiscal 2003 (see Note 14).&#160; Before the cumulative effect adjustment, this
accounting change had the effect of increasing revenue, net income and diluted
earnings per share in fiscal 2003 by $2,451, $1,507 and $0.11,
respectively.&#160; In accordance with
Accounting Principles Board Opinion (APB) No. 20, &#147;Accounting</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">23</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="3" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:12.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>


<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='23',FILE='C:\JMS\kyang\04-10479-1\task184161\10479-1-ba.htm',USER='kyangb',CD='Sep 13 16:16 2004' -->
<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Changes&#148;, pro forma amounts are shown for net income and earnings per
share on the accompanying consolidated statements of income as if this change
in accounting method had been applied for all periods presented.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Goodwill and Other Intangible Assets</font></i></b><b><i><font style="font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;"> &#150; </font></i></b>The cost of intangible
assets with determinable useful lives is amortized to reflect the pattern of
economic benefits consumed, principally on a straight-line basis over the
estimated periods benefited.&#160; The
Company&#146;s policy is to amortize customer relationships with determinable lives
over periods ranging from five to 20 years.&#160;
Contract backlog and other intangibles with contractual terms are
amortized over the respective contractual lives.&#160; Goodwill and indefinite-lived intangibles are
not amortized, but are evaluated at least annually for impairment.&#160; This evaluation is based on valuation models
that incorporate expected future cash flows and profitability projections.&#160; To date, there has been no impairment of
these assets.&#160; Prior to fiscal 2002,
goodwill was amortized over periods not exceeding 40 years.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Property and Equipment</font></i></b><b><i><font style="font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;"> &#150; </font></i></b>Property and equipment are recorded at cost,
including costs to bring the equipment into operation.&#160; Major improvements and betterments to
existing equipment are capitalized.&#160; Maintenance
and repairs are charged to expense as incurred.&#160;
The Company provides for depreciation of property and equipment
utilizing the straight-line method using estimated useful lives of three to 10
years. Leasehold improvements are amortized over the shorter of the lives of
the various leases or the useful lives of the improvements.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Long-Lived Assets &#150; </font></i></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Management
regularly reviews property and equipment and other long-lived assets, including
certain intangibles and goodwill, for possible impairment. This review occurs annually,
or more frequently if events or changes in circumstances indicate the carrying
amount of the asset may not be recoverable. If there is an indication of
impairment of property and equipment or amortizable intangible assets,
management prepares an estimate of future cash flows (undiscounted and without
interest charges) expected to result from the use of the asset and its eventual
disposition. If these cash flows are less than the carrying amount of the
asset, an impairment loss is recognized to write down the asset to its
estimated fair value. The fair value is estimated as the present value of the
future cash flows discounted at a rate commensurate with management&#146;s estimates
of the business risks.&#160; Annually, or
earlier, if there is indication of impairment of intangible assets not subject
to amortization, management compares the estimated fair value with the carrying
amount of the asset. An impairment loss is recognized to write down the
intangible asset to its fair value if it is less than the carrying amount.&#160; Management compares the fair value of the
reporting unit to its carrying value to assess goodwill impairment. If the
estimated fair value of the related reporting unit is less than the carrying
amount, an impairment loss is recognized to write down the goodwill to its
implied fair value.&#160; Preparation of
estimated expected future cash flows is inherently subjective and is based on
management&#146;s best estimate of assumptions concerning expected future conditions.
No impairments were identified as of June&nbsp;30, 2004.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Earnings per Share</font></i></b><b><i><font style="font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;"> &#150; </font></i></b>Earnings per share (EPS) is computed in
accordance with the provisions of Statement of Financial Accounting Standards
(SFAS) No. 128, &#147;Earnings per Share&#148;.&#160;
Basic EPS is based upon net income available to common shareholders
divided by the weighted average common shares outstanding during the year.&#160; Diluted EPS reflects the potential dilutive
effect of outstanding stock options and warrants and the conversion of the
Company&#146;s preferred stock.&#160; For purposes
of computing diluted EPS the Company uses the treasury stock method.&#160; Additionally, when computing dilution related
to the preferred stock, conversion is assumed as of the beginning of the
period.</p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">24</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">For
fiscal 2004 and 2002, assumed conversion of the preferred stock was dilutive,
therefore conversion was assumed for purposes of computing diluted EPS.&#160; For fiscal 2003, assumed conversion of the
preferred stock would have slightly increased rather than decreased EPS (would
have been &#147;anti-dilutive&#148;); therefore conversion was not assumed for purposes
of computing diluted EPS.&#160; As a result,
721 shares were excluded from the calculation of diluted EPS in fiscal 2003.&#160; The following table sets forth the
computations of basic and diluted EPS:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>
